---
title: "Luc's Statistical Coffee Price Forecast"
subtitle: "Monthly Model Report"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: scrartcl
    geometry: "margin=2.5cm, top=2cm"
    colorlinks: true
    fig-pos: "H"
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \usepackage{titlesec}
        \usepackage{fancyhdr}
        \definecolor{coffeeamber}{RGB}{176, 92, 26}
        \definecolor{coffeecream}{RGB}{251, 247, 242}
        \titleformat{\section}{\large\bfseries\color{coffeeamber}}{\thesection}{0.5em}{}[\titlerule]
        \titleformat{\subsection}{\normalsize\bfseries\color{coffeeamber}}{\thesubsection}{0.5em}{}
        \pagestyle{fancy}
        \fancyhf{}
        \fancyhead[L]{\small\textcolor{coffeeamber}{Luc's Statistical Coffee Price Forecast}}
        \fancyhead[R]{\small\textcolor{gray}{\today}}
        \fancyfoot[C]{\small\thepage}
        \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0.4pt}
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
import os
import sqlite3

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import Markdown, display
from tabulate import tabulate

from coffee_forecast.reports import (
    build_forecast_chart,
    build_spread_chart,
    fetch_last_prices,
    fetch_latest_backtest_metrics,
    fetch_latest_forecasts,
    fetch_recent_performance,
    fetch_spread_state,
    fetch_spread_history,
    generate_analysis,
    generate_recent_commentary,
)

db_path = os.environ["REPORT_DB_PATH"]
month = os.environ.get("REPORT_MONTH", "")

conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
forecasts_df = fetch_latest_forecasts(conn)
last_prices = fetch_last_prices(conn)
backtest_df = fetch_latest_backtest_metrics(conn)
spread_state = fetch_spread_state(conn)
spread_hist = fetch_spread_history(conn, months=36)
recent_perf_df = fetch_recent_performance(conn)
conn.close()
```

## Executive Summary

```{python}
analysis = generate_analysis(forecasts_df, last_prices, backtest_df, spread_state)
display(Markdown(analysis))
```

## Current Forecasts

Median (p50) price forecasts and 80% confidence intervals (p10–p90) for the next three months.

```{python}
if forecasts_df.empty:
    display(Markdown("_No forecast data available. Run the pipeline first._"))
else:
    rows = []
    for symbol in ["KC=F", "RM=F"]:
        label = "Arabica (KC=F)" if symbol == "KC=F" else "Robusta (RM=F)"
        sub = forecasts_df[forecasts_df["symbol"] == symbol].sort_values("horizon")
        for _, r in sub.iterrows():
            band = round(float(r["p90"]) - float(r["p10"]), 1)
            rows.append(
                {
                    "Symbol": label,
                    "Horizon": f"h={int(r['horizon'])}",
                    "Median (¢/lb)": f"{r['p50']:.1f}",
                    "80% CI (¢/lb)": f"[{r['p10']:.1f}, {r['p90']:.1f}]",
                    "Band width": f"{band:.1f}",
                }
            )
    display(Markdown(tabulate(rows, headers="keys", tablefmt="pipe")))
```

```{python}
#| label: forecast-chart
#| fig-cap: "Price trajectory with 80% confidence ribbon"
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3.5

fig = build_forecast_chart(forecasts_df, last_prices)
plt.show()
```

\newpage

## Model Performance

Mean absolute error (MAE), mean absolute percentage error (MAPE), and 80% interval coverage across all walk-forward backtest windows.

```{python}
if backtest_df.empty:
    display(Markdown(
        "_No backtest data available. Run `python -m coffee_forecast.models.backtest` first._"
    ))
else:
    display_df = backtest_df.copy()
    display_df["Symbol"] = display_df["symbol"].map(
        {"KC=F": "Arabica", "RM=F": "Robusta"}
    ).fillna(display_df["symbol"])
    display_df["Horizon"] = display_df["horizon"].apply(lambda h: f"h={int(h)}")
    display_df["MAE (¢/lb)"] = display_df["mae"].apply(
        lambda x: f"{x:.1f}" if pd.notna(x) else "N/A"
    )
    display_df["MAPE"] = display_df["mape"].apply(
        lambda x: f"{x:.1f}%" if pd.notna(x) else "N/A"
    )
    display_df["Coverage 80%"] = display_df["coverage_80"].apply(
        lambda x: f"{x*100:.0f}%" if pd.notna(x) else "N/A"
    )
    out = display_df[["Symbol", "Horizon", "MAE (¢/lb)", "MAPE", "Coverage 80%"]]
    display(Markdown(tabulate(out.values.tolist(), headers=out.columns.tolist(), tablefmt="pipe")))
```

**Coverage 80% target:** ≥80% of forecast ranges should contain the realized price.
Values below 80% indicate the model is over-confident.

\newpage

## Recent Forecast Performance

One-month-ahead forecasts versus realized prices over the last three months.

```{python}
if recent_perf_df.empty:
    display(Markdown("_No recent backtest data available._"))
else:
    rows = []
    for _, r in recent_perf_df.iterrows():
        symbol_label = "Arabica (KC=F)" if r["symbol"] == "KC=F" else "Robusta (RM=F)"
        fc = float(r["point_forecast"]) if pd.notna(r["point_forecast"]) else float("nan")
        actual = float(r["actual"]) if pd.notna(r["actual"]) else float("nan")
        err = float(r["error_pct"]) if pd.notna(r["error_pct"]) else float("nan")
        direction = "above" if err > 0 else "below"
        rows.append({
            "Month": str(r["target_date"])[:7],
            "Symbol": symbol_label,
            "Forecast (¢/lb)": f"{fc:.1f}",
            "Actual (¢/lb)": f"{actual:.1f}",
            "Error": f"{'+' if err > 0 else ''}{err:.1f}% ({direction} actual)",
        })
    display(Markdown(tabulate(rows, headers="keys", tablefmt="pipe")))
```

```{python}
commentary = generate_recent_commentary(recent_perf_df)
display(Markdown(commentary))
```

\newpage

## Arabica–Robusta Spread Signal

The spread model tracks whether Arabica is unusually cheap or expensive relative to Robusta.
It measures the **z-score** of the log price ratio — how many standard deviations the current
ratio sits from its long-run average. A z-score near zero means the relationship is normal;
a z-score beyond ±2 means a rare dislocation (this happens roughly 5% of the time historically)
that has tended to revert. A trade signal fires at **|z| > 2** and closes once the ratio
normalises back to **|z| < 0.5**.

```{python}
if not spread_state:
    display(Markdown(
        "_No spread data available. Run `python -m coffee_forecast.models.spread` first._"
    ))
else:
    _sig_map = {
        1: "**Buy Arabica** — Arabica unusually cheap vs Robusta; spread expected to revert upward",
        -1: "**Buy Robusta** — Arabica unusually expensive vs Robusta; spread expected to revert downward",
        0: "**Neutral** — ratio within normal range, no edge detected",
    }
    sig_int = spread_state.get("signal", 0) or 0
    sig_text = _sig_map.get(sig_int, "Neutral")
    z = spread_state.get("z_score", float("nan"))
    hl = spread_state.get("half_life", float("nan"))
    date = spread_state.get("date", "N/A")

    display(Markdown(
        f"| | |\n"
        f"|---|---|\n"
        f"| **As of** | {date} |\n"
        f"| **Current signal** | {sig_text} |\n"
        f"| **Z-score** | {z:.2f} std devs from average |\n"
        f"| **Half-life** | {hl:.1f} months — typical time to revert to average |\n"
        f"| **Entry threshold** | |z| > 2.0 (top/bottom ~5% of history) |\n"
        f"| **Exit threshold** | |z| < 0.5 (back near average) |\n"
    ))
```

```{python}
#| label: spread-chart
#| fig-cap: "Z-score of Arabica/Robusta log spread — last 36 months. Red zone = Arabica expensive; green zone = Arabica cheap. Dashed lines mark entry thresholds (±2 std devs); dotted lines mark exit thresholds (±0.5)."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3

fig_spread = build_spread_chart(spread_hist)
plt.show()
```

\newpage

## Data & Methodology

**Data sources:** Arabica (KC=F) and Robusta (RM=F) monthly prices from FRED
(`PCOFFOTMUSDM`, `PCOFFROBUSDM`). FX drivers (BRL/USD, VND/USD, IDR/USD) from
Alpha Vantage. DXY broad index from FRED (`DTWEXBGS`). Data starts 2000-01-01;
monthly series is the mean of daily adjusted closes within each calendar month.

**VECM:** A two-variable Vector Error Correction Model is fit on log Arabica and
log Robusta prices, with BRL, VND, IDR, and DXY as exogenous drivers. Cointegration
rank is fixed at 1 (confirmed by Engle-Granger test, p=0.009). Lag order is selected
by AIC each run. Point forecasts at 1, 2, and 3 months use a naïve FX assumption
(hold FX at last observed value).

**GAMLSS SHASH:** A Sinh-ArcSinh distribution is fit (via R's `gamlss` package) to
the VECM forecast residuals, separately per symbol and per volatility regime
(Low / Medium / High, defined by rolling 12-month standard deviation). This gives
a full predictive distribution. Final price quantiles are
`price_qX = vecm_point × exp(gamlss_residual_qX)`.

**Spread signal:** An AR(1) model is fit to the log spread series; the expanding
z-score triggers entry at |z| > 2 and exit at |z| < 0.5.

_Report generated by the `coffee-forecast` pipeline._
